zh.lifeSummary: 當Preziosa di Torres於大約1200年出生在義大利薩德尼亞薩薩里阿爾達拉時，她的父親Mariano II de Lacon-Gunale, Judge of Torres20歲，母親Agnese di Massa di Cagliari17歲。她於大約1231年與Niccolò Doria結婚。他們至少是8個兒子的父母。她去世於1231年在義大利利古里亞吉諾瓦享年32歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Preziosa di Torres于大约1200年出生在意大利薩德尼亞薩薩里阿爾達拉时，她的父亲Mariano II de Lacon-Gunale, Judge of Torres20岁，母亲Agnese di Massa di Cagliari17岁。她于大约1231年与Niccolò Doria结婚。他们至少是8个儿子的父母。她去世于1231年在意大利利古里亞吉諾瓦享年32岁。

nameMeanings.name: di
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Mandarin form of the surname 狄, a term referring to the tribes in ancient northern China: (i) from Di (狄), the name of an ancient state (thought to have been located in Hebei or Shandong province), which was granted to Xiao Bo or Kao Bo by King Cheng of Zhou (reigned 1042–1021  BC ). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Ku Di (庫狄) family during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) traced back to other minority ethnic groups in ancient northwestern China.

Mandarin form of the Chinese surname 邸, meaning ‘official residence’ in Chinese: (i) from placename Di (邸), the name of a county (unidentified location). (ii) said to be borne by some descendants of Kujula Kadphises (called 丘就郤 or 邸就郤 in Chinese, 30–c. 80 AD ), founder of the Kushan Empire (one branch of the Yuezhi people originating from western China) located in Central Asia during the Eastern Han dynasty (25–220 AD ).

variants.name: Chi
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Dong, Yun, Cheng, Jae, Yong, Cheng Chung, Chi, Chun, Duk, Fang, Feng.  Korean Chung, Chong, Jae Hong, Moon, Myong, Pong, Shen, Yoo, Byung, Chang, Chol, Chong Hun.  Southeast Asian Phi, Hung, Ly, Tu, Denh, Do Van, Duong, Hao, Kieu, Mui, Nga, Phat.

Chinese: Mandarin and Cantonese form of the surname 池, meaning ‘moat’ in ancient Chinese: (i) acquired as a surname by people who lived beside a moat. (ii) from Chi (池), said to be the name of an ancient state (of unknown location and period). (iii) from the personal name Chi (池), personal name of a son of an official in the state of Qin during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ).

Chinese: Mandarin and Cantonese form of the surname 遲, meaning ‘late’ in Chinese: (i) borne by descendants of Chi Ren (遲任), a person who lived during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). (ii) shortened form of the Chinese compound surname Yu-Chi (尉遲), originally the name of a tribe from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China. (iii) from the second element of the personal name Zi Chi (子遲), style name of Fan Xu, a student of Confucius (551–479  BC ).

nameMeanings.name: Torres
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Luis, Carlos, Manuel, Jesus, Francisco, Pedro, Miguel, Jorge, Raul, Rafael.  Portuguese Ligia, Anatolio, Catarina, Paulo, Albeiro, Gonsalo, Joaquim, Lidio, Omero, Wenceslao.

Galician, Catalan, Spanish, Portuguese, and Jewish (Sephardic): habitational name from any of numerous places called Torres, all named with the plural of torre ‘tower’ (see Torre ). As a Jewish name, it was often adopted at the moment of conversion to Roman Catholicism. After the return to Judaism (generations later), some descendants retained the name their families used as Catholics. Compare De Torres .
Italian: habitational name from Torres in Belluno or Porto Torres in Sassari. In southern Italy the surname is sometimes of Spanish origin (see 1 above).

variants.name: Torrez
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Manuel, Jose, Juan, Pedro, Carlos, Jesus, Armando, Raul, Ruben, Alfredo, Alvaro, Fernando.

variants.name: Torre
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Rocco, Angelo, Salvatore, Carmelo, Vito, Antonio, Carmine, Pietro, Sal, Sebastiano, Agostino, Bartolo.  Spanish Jose, Francisco, Carlos, Julio, Alvaro, Eduardo, Elvira, Ernesto, Fernando, Gregorio, Guillermo, Jorge.

Italian: topographic name for someone who lived near a tower, torre (from Latin turris), usually a defensive fortification or watchtower, or a habitational name from any of the places called with this word, as for example Torre Annunziata or Torre del Greco in Naples province, Torre de’ Passeri (Pescara, Abruzzo), or Torre di Ruggiero (Catanzaro, Calabria). Compare La Torre .

Galician, Catalan, Aragonese, Spanish, and Portuguese: topographic name from torre ‘(watch) tower’ (of the same etymology as 1), or a habitational name from any of numerous places called (La) Torre in Spain and Portugal. Compare Spanish De la Torre and La Torre .

nameMeanings.name: Preziosa
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Aniello, Antonio, Dante, Luigi, Romolo.
Italian: patronymic or plural form of   Prezioso  .

variants.name: Prezioso
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Salvatore, Franco, Antonio, Canio, Carmine, Constantino, Domenica, Fausto, Giovanni, Luigi, Rocco.
Italian: from the adjective prezioso ‘valuable, precious, inestimable’, applied as a nickname and also as a personal name.

en.lifeSummary: When Preziosa di Torres was born about 1200, in Ardara, Sassari, Sardinia, Italy, her father, Mariano II de Lacon-Gunale, Judge of Torres, was 20 and her mother, Agnese di Massa di Cagliari, was 17. She married Niccolò Doria about 1231. They were the parents of at least 8 sons. She died in 1231, in Genova, Liguria, Italy, at the age of 32.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Preziosa di Torres est née environ 1200, à Ardara, Sassari, Sardegna, Italie, son père, Mariano II de Lacon-Gunale, Judge of Torres, avait 20 ans et sa mère, Agnese di Massa di Cagliari, avait 17 ans. Elle a épousé Niccolò Doria environ 1231. Ils ont eu au moins 8 fils. Elle est décédée en 1231, à Genova, Liguria, Italie, à l’âge de 32 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Preziosa di Torres um 1200 in Ardara, Sassari, Sardinien, Italien geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Mariano II de Lacon-Gunale, Judge of Torres 20 und ihre Mutter Agnese di Massa di Cagliari 17. Sie hat um 1231 Niccolò Doria geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 8 Söhne. Sie starb 1231 im Alter von 32 Jahren in Genova, Liguria, Italien.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Preziosa di Torres è nata il circa 1200 nel Ardara, Sassari, Sardegna, Italia, sua padre, Mariano II de Lacon-Gunale, Judge of Torres, aveva 20 anni e sua madre, Agnese di Massa di Cagliari, aveva 17 anni. Lei sposato Niccolò Doria circa 1231. Erano genitori di almeno 8 figli. Morì nel 1231, nel Genova, Liguria, Italia, all'età di 32 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Preziosa di Torresが約1200年にイタリアサルデグナサッサリアルダラで生まれた時，父Mariano II de Lacon-Gunale, Judge of Torresは20歳，母Agnese di Massa di Cagliariは17歳であった。約1231年にNiccolò Doriaと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも8人の息子の親であった。彼女は1231年にイタリアリグリアジェノヴァで32歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Preziosa di Torres(이)가 대략 1200년 이탈리아 사르데그나 사사리 아더라에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Mariano II de Lacon-Gunale, Judge of Torres(은)는 20세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Agnese di Massa di Cagliari(은)는 17세였다. 그녀는 대략 1231년 Niccolò Doria(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여덟 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1231년 이탈리아 리구리아 노바에서 32세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Preziosa di Torres nasceu aproximadamente 1200, em Ardara, Sassari, Sardegna, Itália, seu pai, Mariano II de Lacon-Gunale, Judge of Torres, tinha 20 anos e sua mãe, Agnese di Massa di Cagliari, tinha 17 anos. Ela casou-se com Niccolò Doria aproximadamente 1231. Eles tiveram pelo menos 8 filhos. Ela faleceu em 1231, em Genova, Liguria, Itália, com 32 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Preziosa di Torres родилась около 1200 г. в местности ардара, сассари, сардегна, Италия, её отцу по имени Mariano II de Lacon-Gunale, Judge of Torres было 20 лет, а матери, которую звали Agnese di Massa di Cagliari, 17. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Niccolò Doria около 1231 г. У них было по крайней мере 8 сыновей. Она умерла в 1231 г. в местности дженова, лигуря, Италия в возрасте 32 лет.

sm.de 1220 a 1230: mai le 1220 e o'o I le 1230

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Preziosa di Torres nació aproximadamente 1200, en Ardara, Sassari, Sardegna, Italia, su papá, Mariano II de Lacon-Gunale, Judge of Torres, tenía 20 años y su mamá, Agnese di Massa di Cagliari, tenía 17 años. Se casó con Niccolò Doria aproximadamente 1231. Fueron padres de por lo menos 8 hijos. Murió en 1231, en Genova, Liguria, Italia, a la edad de 32 años.
